channel.title: International Investing on Sakthi Priyan H
channel.description: Recent content in International Investing on Sakthi Priyan H

item.title: FX Retail via Bharat Connect: Private Bank Speed at Public Bank Rates — A Live Transaction Walkthrough
item.description: A step-by-step walkthrough of executing a live INR-to-USD forex transaction using FX Retail via Bharat Connect (BHIM), completing the remittance on ICICI Bank Money2World, and investing in an Irish ETF on IBKR

item.title: 3 Paths to Global Investing for Indians
item.description:
For an Indian investor seeking global diversification, the key decision is not only what to invest in, but how to access international markets.

In practice, there are 3 primary paths,

  • Path 1: Domestic Mutual Funds
  • Path 2: Indian ETFs with International Exposure
  • Path 3: Direct International Investing via LRS

Each path offers different trade-offs in cost, flexibility, and accessibility.

A Quick Comparison

Path Setup Usage Cost Flexibility Taxation Market Access
1. Mutual Funds Very Easy Very Easy Medium Low Low Low
2. Indian ETFs Easy Easy Medium Low Low Low
3. Direct LRS Moderate Moderate Low Very High Medium Very High

Path 1: Domestic Mutual Funds

I have gained exposure to the Nasdaq 100 through two distinct Indian mutual funds. These instruments allow for international diversification using Indian Rupees (INR) without the complexities of direct foreign investing.

item.title: The Global Indian Investor
item.description: A Practical Guide to LRS Remittance, International Brokers, and Building a Global Portfolio with Irish ETFs

item.title: Why Beyond India?
item.description:
The World Has More Than One Market

The global equity universe spans dozens of countries, hundreds of industries, and tens of thousands of companies. India, despite its growth story, is a small slice of that universe. This chapter makes the case for why every serious long-term investor should look beyond the NSE/BSE.

If you invest only in India, you are investing in roughly 3% of the world’s equity markets.
That is not a strategy. That is a blind spot.

item.title: INR to USD: FX Retail Web vs FX Retail Bharat Connect vs Direct Bank Rates
item.description: A comparison of foreign exchange rates for converting INR to USD across different channels like direct banking, FX Retail Web, and the newly discovered FX Retail via Bharat Connect.

item.title: Stop Paying ₹1.5/USD! Cut Forex Markup When Funding IBKR (India Guide)
item.description: How to avoid high forex markups (₹1.5/USD) when funding your Interactive Brokers (IBKR) account from India. Step-by-step guide to save on currency conversion costs.

item.title: Using Form 12BAA to Reduce Cashflow Drag on International Investments
item.description: How salaried Indians investing internationally via LRS can use Form 12BAA to reduce cashflow drag from TCS, optimize compounding, and avoid capital lock-in.

item.title: RealValue FX Engine
item.description: Calculate your true effective FX rate including hidden spreads and taxes, compare quotes across banks to minimize costs, track your ₹10L LRS limit via a browser-only transaction log, and compute your TCS refund opportunity cost.

item.title: How to Invest in NASDAQ 100 from India (Mutual Funds, ETFs, and IBKR Guide)
item.description: Options to invest in the NASDAQ 100 index from India — covering mutual funds, Indian ETFs, and the LRS route via Interactive Brokers — along with a comparison of Indian apps vs IBKR with UCITS ETFs, buying cost, and taxation.

item.title: Cut Bank Forex Markup on INR to USD Using FX Retail | IBKR Funding, Forex Card, LRS
item.description: Indian investors lose 2-3% to hidden bank forex markup on every INR to USD transfer. Learn how to use FX Retail to negotiate lower rates for IBKR funding, forex card loading, and LRS remittances.

item.title: Funding Interactive Brokers from India Using FX Retail
item.description: How Indian investors can reduce the hidden forex markup cost when funding Interactive Brokers accounts by using FX Retail instead of the bank’s default remittance rate.

item.title: Invest in NASDAQ 100 from India
item.description: Options to invest in NASDAQ 100, process, cost and taxation

item.title: Invest in NASDAQ 100 from India
item.description: Complete guide on how Indian investors can invest in NASDAQ 100 index and benefit from US market growth.
